description: Facing a single open from a non-blind seat: 3-bet (value+bluff) / call / fold, by hero position x opener position. Linear/value-heavy per low-stakes research.

actions.combos: TT, JJ, AQs, AJs, KQs, KJs, QJs

entries.rationale: UTG opens the strongest range at the table and UTG+1 sits directly behind it with the whole field still to act, so this is the tightest vs-RFI spot in the pack: 3-bet only genuine value (QQ+, AK) plus the A5s blocker-bluff, and flat a narrow high-equity band (TT-JJ, AQs-AJs, suited Broadways) — dominated offsuit hands and thin suited hands can't continue profitably against a range this strong, and cold-calling with 5+ players still to act invites a squeeze, so the flat stays disciplined.

actions.combos: 99, TT, JJ, AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs

entries.rationale: UTG+2 sits one seat closer to the button than UTG+1, so its continuing range widens a touch: the pair floor drops to 99 and the call range picks up ATs/JTs, since one fewer player behind means less squeeze exposure — the 3-bet range stays the same genuine-value-plus-blocker shape because UTG's open is still the strongest range at the table.

actions.combos: 99, TT, JJ, AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs

entries.rationale: UTG+1's open is only a hair wider than UTG's and still EP-strong, so UTG+2 re-values essentially as it does facing UTG: 3-bet QQ+/AK plus the A5s blocker, flat the same 99-JJ/suited-Broadway band — the one-notch-wider opener doesn't justify loosening further against a value-heavy pool that rarely opens EP light.

actions.combos: 77-JJ, AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs

entries.rationale: UTG's range is the strongest at the table, so HJ 3-bets only genuine value (QQ+, AK) plus the A5s blocker bluff and calls a tight set of pairs and suited broadways — dominated offsuit hands and thin suited hands don't have enough equity to continue profitably against a range this strong.

actions.combos: 66-JJ, AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs, T9s

entries.rationale: CO's extra seat of position over HJ lets the calling range widen down to 66 and add T9s: the players who could squeeze behind CO have already folded, so speculative suited hands can realize more of their equity in position against UTG's tight range.

actions.combos: 66-TT, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs, AQo

entries.rationale: HJ opens wider than UTG, so CO's continuing range loosens to match: the 3-bet threshold drops to JJ/AQs and the call range picks up AQo, since a looser opener makes offsuit broadway hands profitable to continue with in position.

actions.combos: TT+, AQs, AKs, AKo, A5s, A4s
22-99, AJs, ATs, A9s, KJs, KTs, QJs, QTs, JTs, T9s, 98s, AQo, AJo, KQo

entries.rationale: CO's open is wide enough that BTN can 3-bet down to TT and call with any pair down to 22 plus offsuit broadways like AJo/KQo — BTN's positional advantage lets these speculative and dominated-adjacent hands realize their equity postflop that they couldn't from an earlier seat.

actions.combos: 22-TT, AJs, ATs, KJs, QJs, JTs, T9s, AQo

entries.rationale: HJ's open is tighter than CO's, so BTN's continuing range shrinks accordingly: the 3-bet threshold rises to JJ+ and the weakest suited connectors drop out of the call range, since a stronger opening range makes those speculative calls less profitable even in position.

Facing the tightest possible open, BTN's continuing range is its narrowest vs-RFI spot: only QQ+/AK 3-bet for value and a handful of pairs plus AQs/AJs/KQs call, because UTG's range is strong enough that even BTN's positional edge doesn't rescue weaker holdings.

actions.combos: 88-JJ, AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs

entries.rationale: LJ sits between UTG+2 and HJ, so facing UTG's tightest-at-the-table open its continuing range interpolates exactly between them: 3-bet only genuine value (QQ+, AK) plus the A5s blocker-bluff, and call the pair floor 88 — one notch wider than UTG+2's 99 and one tighter than HJ's 77 — plus the same AQs-ATs/suited-Broadway band, because each seat closer to the button faces one fewer potential squeezer behind. Doc 05 §3.3 gap-fill, copied verbatim; monotonic with the authored UTG1/UTG2/HJ-vs-UTG chain.

actions.combos: 88-JJ, AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs

entries.rationale: UTG+1's open is only a hair wider than UTG's and still EP-strong, so LJ re-values exactly as it does facing UTG: 3-bet QQ+/AK plus the A5s blocker, call the 88-JJ/suited-Broadway band — the one-notch-wider opener doesn't justify loosening further against a value-heavy pool that rarely opens EP light (the same equal-shape treatment the authored UTG2-vs-UTG1 entry uses).

actions.combos: 88-JJ, AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs

entries.rationale: UTG+2 is the widest of the three EP opens but still a value-lean EP range (its only additions over UTG+1 are 98s and KQo), so LJ keeps the same disciplined continue it uses against UTG/UTG+1: 3-bet QQ+/AK plus the A5s blocker, call 88-JJ and the suited Broadways — loosening past this vs a range that's ~95% the same hands would just add dominated calls.

actions.combos: 77-JJ, AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs

entries.rationale: UTG+1 opens essentially the same strength tier as UTG (one hand class wider), so HJ continues exactly as it does facing UTG: 3-bet only genuine value (QQ+, AK) plus the A5s blocker bluff and call the tight 77-JJ/suited-Broadway set — mirrors the authored HJ-vs-UTG entry, per the UTG2-vs-UTG1 equal-shape precedent for near-identical EP openers.

actions.combos: 77-JJ, AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs

entries.rationale: UTG+2's open is the EP-MP transition range but still value-lean, so HJ keeps its vs-UTG shape: 3-bet QQ+/AK plus the A5s blocker, call 77-JJ and the suited Broadways — the marginal extra width of the UTG+2 open (98s, KQo) doesn't make any new HJ hand a profitable continue with four players still behind.

actions.combos: 77-TT, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs, AQo

entries.rationale: LJ's open is meaningfully wider than the EP seats', so HJ's continuing range loosens to match: the 3-bet threshold drops to JJ/AQs and AQo enters the call range, mirroring the authored CO-vs-HJ shape one opener earlier. It stays a touch tighter than CO-vs-LJ (no 66, no T9s) because HJ still has three seats plus the blinds behind it. Doc 05 §3.3's optional fourth entry (mirror CO-vs-LJ, a touch tighter).

actions.combos: 66-JJ, AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs, T9s

entries.rationale: UTG+1's open is a near-copy of UTG's, so CO continues exactly as it does facing UTG: 3-bet QQ+/AK plus the A5s blocker, call down to 66 and add T9s — CO's late-position edge (only BTN and the blinds behind) lets the speculative suited hands realize their equity against an EP range. Mirrors the authored CO-vs-UTG entry, per the UTG2-vs-UTG1 equal-shape precedent.

actions.combos: 66-JJ, AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs, T9s

entries.rationale: UTG+2 opens the widest of the EP seats but still a value-lean range, so CO keeps its vs-UTG continue: 3-bet QQ+/AK plus the A5s blocker, call 66-JJ with the suited Broadways and T9s — the opener's marginal extra width doesn't shift any CO hand across the profitability line, and squeeze exposure from BTN and the blinds is unchanged.

actions.combos: 66-TT, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs, T9s, AQo

entries.rationale: LJ opens wider than the EP seats, so CO's continuing range loosens the way the authored CO-vs-HJ entry does — 3-bet threshold down to JJ/AQs, AQo enters the call — while keeping T9s from its vs-UTG call (positional continuation of a speculative suited hand). Doc 05 §3.3 gap-fill, copied verbatim; sits between CO-vs-UTG (tighter 3-bets) and CO-vs-HJ (drops nothing) exactly as LJ sits between those openers.

UTG+1's open is essentially UTG's range one hand class wider, so BTN treats it identically to the authored BTN-vs-UTG entry: only QQ+/AK 3-bet for value and the narrow 99-JJ/AQs/AJs/KQs band calls — even BTN's positional edge doesn't rescue weaker holdings against an EP range this strong. Equal-shape treatment per the UTG2-vs-UTG1 precedent.

UTG+2's open is still a value-lean EP range (its width over UTG+1 is just 98s and KQo), so BTN keeps the same narrow vs-UTG continue: 3-bet QQ+/AK, call 99-JJ/AQs/AJs/KQs — the marginally wider opener doesn't make BTN's next-best hands (88, ATs, KJs) profitable continues against a range that is still ~14% and value-heavy.

actions.combos: 22-TT, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs, T9s, AQo

entries.rationale: LJ's open is wide enough that BTN plays it like a slightly tighter HJ open: 3-bet down to JJ/AQs with the A5s blocker, and call any pair (22-TT set-mines profitably in position with no squeeze risk behind but the blinds) plus the suited-Broadway/T9s/AQo band. Doc 05 §3.3 gap-fill, copied verbatim; nests between the authored BTN-vs-UTG (much tighter) and BTN-vs-HJ (adds KQs here vs the wider LJ-defense) rows.
